area: LLM/provider wiring + config (tradingagents/llm_clients/, default_config.py, .env examples, plus the graph/CLI code that consumes them)
summary:
ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW The subsystem is a thin provider-abstraction layer: a factory returns a per-provider "client" object whose only job is to construct a configured LangChain chat model. The graph layer creates exactly TWO chat models per run â€” a "quick_think" model and a "deep_think" model â€” from the same provider, and hands them to agent factory functions. 1. FACTORY (tradingagents/llm_clients/factory.py) Signature: `create_llm_client(provider: str, model: str, base_url: Optional[str] = None, **kwargs) -> BaseLLMClient`. Routing (provider lowercased, imports are lazy so missing SDKs/keys don't break import): - `_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE = ("openai", "xai", "deepseek", "qwen", "glm", "ollama", "openrouter")` -> `OpenAIClient(model, base_url, provider=provider_lower, **kwargs)` - "anthropic" -> `AnthropicClient(model, base_url, **kwargs)` - "google" -> `GoogleClient(model, base_url, **kwargs)` - "azure" -> `AzureOpenAIClient(model, base_url, **kwargs)` - else `ValueError(f"Unsupported LLM provider: {provider}")`. Public API via `tradingagents/llm_clients/__init__.py`: only `BaseLLMClient` and `create_llm_client`. 2. BASE CLASS (base_client.py) `BaseLLMClient(ABC)` stores `self.model`, `self.base_url`, `self.kwargs`. Abstract: `get_llm() -> Any` (returns the LangChain chat model) and `validate_model() -> bool`. `warn_if_unknown_model()` emits a non-fatal `RuntimeWarning` ("Model '' is not in the known model list for provider '

'. Continuing anyway.") â€” validation never blocks. Module-level `normalize_content(response)`: if `response.content` is a list of typed blocks (Responses API / Gemini 3 / Claude extended thinking), it keeps only dict blocks with `type == "text"` (taking their "text") plus bare strings, joins non-empty pieces with "\n", and assigns back to `response.content`. Every provider has a `Normalized*` subclass whose `invoke()` wraps `super().invoke()` with this. 3. OPENAI-COMPATIBLE CLIENT (openai_client.py) `OpenAIClient.__init__(model, base_url=None, provider="openai", **kwargs)`. `get_llm()` builds kwargs: - Provider table `_PROVIDER_CONFIG = {"xai": ("https://api.x.ai/v1", "XAI_API_KEY"), "deepseek": ("https://api.deepseek.com", "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"), "qwen": ("https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1", "DASHSCOPE_API_KEY"), "glm": ("https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/", "ZHIPU_API_KEY"), "openrouter": ("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"), "ollama": ("http://localhost:11434/v1", None)}`. For these providers: `base_url = self.base_url or default_base` (explicit base_url, e.g. corporate proxy, wins); api key read via `os.environ.get(api_key_env)` and passed only if non-empty; ollama gets literal `api_key="ollama"`. - For provider "openai": no table entry; `base_url` passed only if explicitly set; `use_responses_api=True` is forced (native OpenAI uses /v1/responses so reasoning_effort works with function tools); `OPENAI_API_KEY` is read implicitly by langchain_openai/ChatOpenAI unless `api_key` arrives via passthrough kwargs. - Passthrough kwargs forwarded verbatim when present: `("timeout", "max_retries", "reasoning_effort", "api_key", "callbacks", "http_client", "http_async_client")`. - Class selection: `DeepSeekChatOpenAI if self.provider == "deepseek" else NormalizedChatOpenAI`. `NormalizedChatOpenAI(ChatOpenAI)`: normalizes invoke output AND overrides `with_structured_output(schema, *, method=None, **kwargs)` to default `method="function_calling"` (avoids the Responses-API parse path's noisy PydanticSerializationUnexpectedValue warnings). `DeepSeekChatOpenAI(NormalizedChatOpenAI)` has two quirks: (a) thinking-mode round-trip â€” `_create_chat_result` copies `choice["message"]["reasoning_content"]` into `generation.message.additional_kwargs["reasoning_content"]` on receive, and `_get_request_payload` re-attaches `message_dict["reasoning_content"]` for outgoing AIMessages (otherwise DeepSeek API returns HTTP 400); helper `_input_to_messages(input_)` handles both bare message lists and ChatPromptValue (`.to_messages()`); (b) `with_structured_output` raises `NotImplementedError` when `self.model_name == "deepseek-reasoner"` (no tool_choice support) â€” agent factories catch this and fall back to free-text generation (see tradingagents/agents/utils/structured.py). 4. ANTHROPIC CLIENT (anthropic_client.py) `NormalizedChatAnthropic(ChatAnthropic)` normalizes invoke content (Claude extended thinking/tool use returns block lists). `get_llm()` passes `model`, `base_url` if set, and passthrough kwargs `("timeout", "max_retries", "api_key", "max_tokens", "callbacks", "http_client", "http_async_client", "effort")`. Note `effort` (Claude 4.5+/4.6 effort control) is forwarded straight into ChatAnthropic. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is read implicitly by langchain_anthropic â€” never read in this repo's code. 5. GOOGLE CLIENT (google_client.py) `NormalizedChatGoogleGenerativeAI(ChatGoogleGenerativeAI)`. Passthrough: `("timeout", "max_retries", "callbacks", "http_client", "http_async_client")`. Unified key mapping: `google_api_key = self.kwargs.get("api_key") or self.kwargs.get("google_api_key")` -> passed as `google_api_key`; otherwise `GOOGLE_API_KEY` env read implicitly by the SDK. Thinking mapping from kwarg `thinking_level`: if "gemini-3" in model name -> pass `thinking_level` through, except Gemini 3 *Pro* doesn't support "minimal" so it is coerced to "low"; non-gemini-3 (i.e. 2.5) -> `thinking_budget = -1 if thinking_level == "high" else 0`. 6. AZURE CLIENT (azure_client.py) `AzureOpenAIClient.get_llm()` builds `{"model": self.model, "azure_deployment": os.environ.get("AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME", self.model)}` plus passthrough `("timeout", "max_retries", "api_key", "reasoning_effort", "callbacks", "http_client", "http_async_client")`, returns `NormalizedAzureChatOpenAI`. Required env (read implicitly by AzureChatOpenAI except deployment name): `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY`, `AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, `AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME`, `OPENAI_API_VERSION`. `validate_model()` always True (any deployment name). 7. MODEL CATALOG + VALIDATION (model_catalog.py, validators.py) `MODEL_OPTIONS: Dict[provider, Dict[mode, List[(display, model_id)]]]` with modes exactly "quick" and "deep" â€” this single dict drives both CLI menus and validation. Providers: openai (quick: gpt-5.4-mini/gpt-5.4-nano/gpt-5.4/gpt-4.1; deep: gpt-5.4/gpt-5.2/gpt-5.4-mini/gpt-5.4-pro), anthropic (quick: claude-sonnet-4-6/claude-haiku-4-5/claude-sonnet-4-5; deep: claude-opus-4-6/claude-opus-4-5/claude-sonnet-4-6/claude-sonnet-4-5), google (gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview, gemini-2.5-flash-lite / deep: gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash), xai (grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning etc., deep flagship grok-4-0709), deepseek (quick: deepseek-v4-flash, deepseek-chat, "custom"; deep: deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-reasoner, deepseek-chat, "custom"), qwen, glm, ollama. OpenRouter is fetched dynamically (not in catalog); Azure accepts any name. `get_model_options(provider, mode)` and `get_known_models()` (flattens to provider -> sorted set of ids, including the sentinel "custom"). validators.py: `VALID_MODELS` = known models minus ollama/openrouter; `validate_model(provider, model)` returns True for ollama/openrouter, True for providers not in the dict, else membership test. Failures only warn (see base class). 8. CONFIG (tradingagents/default_config.py â€” DEFAULT_CONFIG, a plain dict) Keys (exact): `project_dir`; `results_dir` (env `TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR`, default ~/.tradingagents/logs); `data_cache_dir` (env `TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR`, default ~/.tradingagents/cache); `memory_log_path` (env `TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH`, default ~/.tradingagents/memory/trading_memory.md); `memory_log_max_entries: None`; `llm_provider: "openai"`; `deep_think_llm: "gpt-5.4"`; `quick_think_llm: "gpt-5.4-mini"`; `backend_url: None` (deliberately None â€” a comment warns that keeping a provider-specific URL here leaked OpenAI's /v1 into Gemini requests; the CLI sets it per provider); `google_thinking_level: None`; `openai_reasoning_effort: None`; `anthropic_effort: None`; `checkpoint_enabled: False`; `output_language: "English"` (reports only; internal debate stays English); `max_debate_rounds: 1`; `max_risk_discuss_rounds: 1`; `max_recur_limit: 100`; `data_vendors` ({core_stock_apis, technical_indicators, fundamental_data, news_data} each "yfinance"|"alpha_vantage"); `tool_vendors` ({} â€” per-tool override beats category). Quirk: `max_recur_limit` is defined in config but `TradingAgentsGraph` constructs `Propagator()` with no args, so the LangGraph `recursion_limit` is always the constructor default 100 (tradingagents/graph/propagation.py line 64: `config = {"recursion_limit": self.max_recur_limit}`) â€” the config key is effectively dead. 9. GRAPH WIRING â€” quick vs deep split (tradingagents/graph/trading_graph.py + setup.py) `TradingAgentsGraph.__init__(selected_analysts=["market","social","news","fundamentals"], debug=False, config=None, callbacks=None)` creates two clients with identical provider/base_url: `create_llm_client(provider=config["llm_provider"], model=config["deep_think_llm"], base_url=config.get("backend_url"), **llm_kwargs)` and the same with `config["quick_think_llm"]`; then `.get_llm()` on each. `_get_provider_kwargs()` translates config -> client kwargs: provider "google": `google_thinking_level` -> `thinking_level`; "openai": `openai_reasoning_effort` -> `reasoning_effort`; "anthropic": `anthropic_effort` -> `effort`. If `callbacks` given they're added to `llm_kwargs["callbacks"]` (bound at LLM construction, so they fire for every call by either model). GraphSetup (setup.py) assignment â€” QUICK model: `create_market_analyst`, `create_social_media_analyst`, `create_news_analyst`, `create_fundamentals_analyst` (each paired with a ToolNode and a `create_msg_delete()` cleanup node), `create_bull_researcher`, `create_bear_researcher`, `create_trader`, `create_aggressive_debator`, `create_neutral_debator`, `create_conservative_debator`. DEEP model: only `create_research_manager` and `create_portfolio_manager`. Additionally quick model goes to `Reflector(self.quick_thinking_llm)` and `SignalProcessor(self.quick_thinking_llm)` (the latter no longer calls the LLM at all â€” deterministic `parse_rating` on the PM's structured markdown which always carries a `**Rating**: X` header). Graph node names (exact): "Market Analyst"/"Social Analyst"... via `f"{analyst_type.capitalize()} Analyst"`, "Msg Clear ", "tools_", "Bull Researcher", "Bear Researcher", "Research Manager", "Trader", "Aggressive Analyst", "Neutral Analyst", "Conservative Analyst", "Portfolio Manager". Flow: analysts sequential (each loops analyst<->tools until no tool_calls, then msg-clear) -> Bull/Bear alternate -> Research Manager -> Trader -> Aggressive -> Conservative -> Neutral cycle -> Portfolio Manager -> END. Round counts (tradingagents/graph/conditional_logic.py): investment debate ends when `investment_debate_state["count"] >= 2 * max_debate_rounds` (2 agents per round; next speaker decided by whether `current_response` startswith "Bull"); risk debate ends when `risk_debate_state["count"] >= 3 * max_risk_discuss_rounds` (3 agents per round; rotation keyed off `latest_speaker` startswith "Aggressive"/"Conservative"). CLI "research depth" sets BOTH max_debate_rounds and max_risk_discuss_rounds to the same value: Shallow=1, Medium=3, Deep=5. 10. CLI PROVIDER/BASE_URL SELECTION (cli/utils.py, cli/main.py) `select_llm_provider()` returns `(provider_key, base_url)` from a hardcoded list: OpenAI -> "https://api.openai.com/v1"; Google -> None; Anthropic -> "https://api.anthropic.com/"; xAI -> "https://api.x.ai/v1"; DeepSeek -> "https://api.deepseek.com"; Qwen -> "https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1" (NOTE: mainland URL, differs from the client default dashscope-intl); GLM -> "https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/" (differs from client default api.z.ai); OpenRouter -> "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"; Azure -> None; Ollama -> "http://localhost:11434/v1". This URL is stored as `config["backend_url"]` and, being non-None, overrides the client-table default for OpenAI-compatible providers. Model pickers `select_shallow_thinking_agent(provider)` / `select_deep_thinking_agent(provider)` call `_select_model(provider, "quick"|"deep")` over `get_model_options`; "custom" sentinel prompts for free-text model id; OpenRouter fetches live model list from GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (unauthenticated, 10s timeout, top 5 shown); Azure prompts for a deployment name. Provider-specific step 8: google -> `ask_gemini_thinking_config()` ("high"|"minimal"); openai -> `ask_openai_reasoning_effort()` ("medium"|"high"|"low"); anthropic -> `ask_anthropic_effort()` ("high"|"medium"|"low"). `run_analysis()` copies DEFAULT_CONFIG and sets: max_debate_rounds, max_risk_discuss_rounds, quick_think_llm, deep_think_llm, backend_url, llm_provider, google_thinking_level, openai_reasoning_effort, anthropic_effort, output_language, checkpoint_enabled, then builds `TradingAgentsGraph(..., debug=True, callbacks=[stats_handler])`. 11. TOKEN/COST TRACKING (cli/stats_handler.py) `StatsCallbackHandler(BaseCallbackHandler)` â€” thread-safe (threading.Lock) counters only, NO cost/pricing computation: `llm_calls` incremented in both `on_llm_start` and `on_chat_model_start`; `tool_calls` in `on_tool_start`; `on_llm_end` pulls `response.generations[0][0].message.usage_metadata` (AIMessage) and adds `usage_metadata.get("input_tokens", 0)` / `get("output_tokens", 0)` to `tokens_in`/`tokens_out`. `get_stats()` returns the 4-key dict. It is bound at LLM construction via the `callbacks` passthrough kwarg, and the CLI footer renders these live. The non-CLI entrypoint (main.py) uses no callbacks. 12. ENV/DOTENV Root main.py: `load_dotenv()`. cli/main.py: `load_dotenv()` then `load_dotenv(".env.enterprise", override=False)`. .env.example lists exactly: OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, DASHSCOPE_API_KEY, ZHIPU_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY (all empty). .env.enterprise.example: AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT (https://your-resource-name.openai.azure.com/), AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, optional OPENAI_API_VERSION (commented, e.g. 2024-10-21). 13. EVERY API-KEY READ SITE (runtime code, excluding build\lib and tradingagents-cc) Explicit `os.environ`/`os.getenv`: - openai_client.py:154 `os.environ.get(api_key_env)` for XAI_API_KEY / DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / DASHSCOPE_API_KEY / ZHIPU_API_KEY / OPENROUTER_API_KEY (skipped if unset â€” then ChatOpenAI falls back to implicit OPENAI_API_KEY lookup, a subtle failure mode). - azure_client.py:41 `os.environ.get("AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME", self.model)`. - tradingagents/dataflows/alpha_vantage_common.py:12 `os.getenv("ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY")` â€” the ONLY hard-failing key (`raise ValueError("ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY environment variable is not set.")`), used as `"apikey"` query param; only hit when a data vendor is set to "alpha_vantage" (default is yfinance, keyless). Implicit (inside LangChain SDK constructors, no repo code reads them): OPENAI_API_KEY (ChatOpenAI), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (ChatAnthropic), GOOGLE_API_KEY (ChatGoogleGenerativeAI), AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY + AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT + OPENAI_API_VERSION (AzureChatOpenAI). An `api_key` can also be injected programmatically through the passthrough-kwargs path on openai/anthropic/azure clients and as unified `api_key` -> `google_api_key` on google. tests/conftest.py monkeypatches all 10 key env vars to "placeholder" and patches `tradingagents.llm_clients.factory.create_llm_client` so tests never touch real APIs.

key_components.role: create_llm_client(provider, model, base_url=None, **kwargs) â€” routes 10 provider names to 4 client classes; _OPENAI_COMPATIBLE tuple; lazy imports; ValueError on unknown provider
BaseLLMClient ABC (get_llm/validate_model), warn_if_unknown_model (RuntimeWarning, non-blocking), normalize_content() that flattens typed-block content lists to a newline-joined string of text blocks

OpenAIClient for openai/xai/deepseek/qwen/glm/ollama/openrouter; _PROVIDER_CONFIG (default base_url + API-key env var per provider); use_responses_api=True for native OpenAI; NormalizedChatOpenAI (with_structured_output defaults to function_calling); DeepSeekChatOpenAI (reasoning_content round-trip, deepseek-reasoner structured-output NotImplementedError)
AnthropicClient -> NormalizedChatAnthropic(ChatAnthropic); passthrough kwargs include 'effort' and 'max_tokens'; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY read implicitly by langchain_anthropic
GoogleClient; unified api_key -> google_api_key mapping; thinking_level mapping (gemini-3 passthrough with Pro minimal->low coercion; gemini-2.5 -> thinking_budget -1/0)
AzureOpenAIClient; reads AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME explicitly (fallback: model name); relies on AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY/ENDPOINT/OPENAI_API_VERSION env; validate_model always True
MODEL_OPTIONS dict keyed provider -> {'quick': [...], 'deep': [...]} of (display, model_id) tuples; single source for CLI menus and validators; get_model_options / get_known_models
validate_model(provider, model): membership check against catalog; always True for ollama/openrouter/unknown providers; violations only warn

DEFAULT_CONFIG dict: llm_provider/deep_think_llm/quick_think_llm/backend_url, three provider thinking keys, max_debate_rounds/max_risk_discuss_rounds/max_recur_limit, checkpoint_enabled, output_language, paths (3 TRADINGAGENTS_* env overrides), data_vendors/tool_vendors

TradingAgentsGraph: builds deep+quick clients via create_llm_client with config['backend_url']; _get_provider_kwargs() maps google_thinking_level->thinking_level, openai_reasoning_effort->reasoning_effort, anthropic_effort->effort; binds callbacks into LLM constructors

GraphSetup(quick_thinking_llm, deep_thinking_llm, tool_nodes, conditional_logic): quick LLM -> 4 analysts + bull/bear researchers + trader + 3 risk debators; deep LLM -> Research Manager + Portfolio Manager only
Debate termination: investment count >= 2*max_debate_rounds; risk count >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds; speaker rotation by string-prefix of last speaker
Reflector(quick_thinking_llm): single quick-LLM call reflect_on_final_decision(final_decision, raw_return, alpha_return) with a fixed 2-4-sentence prose system prompt for the memory log
SignalProcessor: accepts quick LLM for backwards compat but makes NO LLM call; deterministic parse_rating extracts the 5-tier rating from PM's '**Rating**: X' markdown

select_llm_provider() -> (provider_key, base_url) hardcoded tuples (CLI Qwen/GLM URLs differ from client defaults); _select_model(provider, 'quick'|'deep'); ask_openai_reasoning_effort / ask_anthropic_effort / ask_gemini_thinking_config; research depth 1/3/5; OpenRouter live model fetch
load_dotenv() + load_dotenv('.env.enterprise', override=False); run_analysis() copies DEFAULT_CONFIG, writes 11 keys from selections, passes callbacks=[StatsCallbackHandler()] and debug=True
StatsCallbackHandler: thread-safe llm_calls/tool_calls/tokens_in/tokens_out counters from AIMessage.usage_metadata; counts only, no cost math
get_api_key(): os.getenv('ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY'), raises ValueError if unset â€” only hard-required key in the codebase, and only when alpha_vantage vendor is selected
Lists the 8 LLM-provider key env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, DASHSCOPE_API_KEY, ZHIPU_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY
Azure vars: AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, optional OPENAI_API_VERSION
Library entrypoint example: load_dotenv(); overrides deep/quick_think_llm to gpt-5.4-mini; no callbacks

llm_touchpoints: Market Analyst (create_market_analyst) â€” QUICK model, bound to ToolNode [get_stock_data, get_indicators]; loops analyst<->tools until no tool_calls (conditional should_continue_market)
Social Media Analyst (create_social_media_analyst) â€” QUICK model, ToolNode [get_news]
News Analyst (create_news_analyst) â€” QUICK model, ToolNode [get_news, get_global_news, get_insider_transactions]
Fundamentals Analyst (create_fundamentals_analyst) â€” QUICK model, ToolNode [get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement]
Bull Researcher / Bear Researcher (create_bull_researcher / create_bear_researcher) â€” QUICK model, free-text debate turns; alternate until investment_debate_state.count >= 2*max_debate_rounds
Research Manager (create_research_manager) â€” DEEP model; structured output (one of the structured-output trio per recent commits), judges the bull/bear debate
Trader (create_trader) â€” QUICK model; structured output
Aggressive / Neutral / Conservative debators (create_aggressive_debator etc.) â€” QUICK model, free text; rotate until risk_debate_state.count >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds

Portfolio Manager (create_portfolio_manager) â€” DEEP model; typed PortfolioDecision structured output rendered to markdown with a '**Rating**: X' header (5-tier: Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell)

Reflector.reflect_on_final_decision â€” QUICK model, no tools; fixed system prompt: 'You are a trading analyst reviewing your own past decision now that the outcome is known. Write exactly 2-4 sentences of plain prose...' with human message carrying raw return, alpha vs SPY, and the final decision; output stored verbatim in the memory log
SignalProcessor.process_signal â€” NO LLM call (deterministic parse_rating); quick LLM accepted for backwards compatibility only

Structured-output mechanics: NormalizedChatOpenAI.with_structured_output forces method='function_calling'; DeepSeekChatOpenAI raises NotImplementedError for deepseek-reasoner and agent factories fall back to free-text (tradingagents/agents/utils/structured.py)
external_dependencies: Exactly one LLM provider API is required per run; key env vars by provider: OPENAI_API_KEY (implicit via ChatOpenAI), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (implicit via ChatAnthropic), GOOGLE_API_KEY (implicit), XAI_API_KEY / DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / DASHSCOPE_API_KEY (Qwen) / ZHIPU_API_KEY (GLM) / OPENROUTER_API_KEY (explicit os.environ.get in openai_client.py), AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY + AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT + AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME + OPENAI_API_VERSION (Azure). Ollama needs no key (api_key='ollama', http://localhost:11434/v1) â€” the only fully keyless LLM path today

yfinance (default for all 4 data-vendor categories) â€” no API key, network access to Yahoo Finance required; also used directly in trading_graph._fetch_returns (ticker + SPY history) for memory-log outcome resolution
Alpha Vantage â€” OPTIONAL alternative data vendor; ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY required (hard ValueError) only if any data_vendors/tool_vendors entry is set to 'alpha_vantage'
OpenRouter model listing â€” OPTIONAL, CLI only: unauthenticated GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models, 10s timeout, graceful degradation to manual model-id entry
python-dotenv â€” loads .env (and .env.enterprise with override=False in the CLI)
TRADINGAGENTS_RESULTS_DIR / TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR / TRADINGAGENTS_MEMORY_LOG_PATH env vars â€” optional path overrides, default under ~/.tradingagents/
port_notes: PRESERVE: (1) The two-tier quick_think/deep_think split and its exact agent assignment â€” quick for the 4 tool-using analysts, bull/bear researchers, trader, 3 risk debators, and the reflection call; deep ONLY for Research Manager and Portfolio Manager. In Claude Agent SDK terms this maps cleanly to model selection per agent/subagent (e.g. a Haiku/Sonnet-class model for quick, Sonnet/Opus-class for deep) â€” keep config keys quick_think_llm/deep_think_llm so the split survives. (2) Round-count semantics exactly: investment debate terminates at count >= 2*max_debate_rounds, risk debate at count >= 3*max_risk_discuss_rounds, with the string-prefix speaker rotation ('Bull'/'Aggressive'/'Conservative'); CLI depth presets 1/3/5 set both knobs. (3) The stats contract {llm_calls, tool_calls, tokens_in, tokens_out} â€” reimplement from the SDK's per-message usage info instead of LangChain callbacks; it is counts only, no pricing. (4) Config keys consumed by the graph: llm_provider, deep_think_llm, quick_think_llm, backend_url, anthropic_effort, checkpoint_enabled, output_language, max_debate_rounds, max_risk_discuss_rounds, results_dir, data_cache_dir, memory_log_path, data_vendors/tool_vendors. (5) Reflector's exact prompt and the PM '**Rating**: X' 5-tier parse (no LLM needed for signal processing). SIMPLIFY/DELETE: the entire multi-provider factory collapses â€” with subscription auth the Claude Agent SDK uses the logged-in Claude Code credentials, so NO API key env vars are read at all; delete _PROVIDER_CONFIG, all os.environ key reads, .env.example LLM keys, google/azure/openai clients, validators+model_catalog (or trim the catalog to an Anthropic-only quick/deep menu), and normalize_content (the SDK returns assembled text; if you read content blocks yourself, you still need the 'keep only text blocks' rule when extended thinking is on). backend_url/base_url selection becomes unnecessary (or maps to ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL only if a gateway is ever needed). anthropic_effort ('high'|'medium'|'low', currently forwarded as ChatAnthropic effort=) maps to the SDK's effort/thinking budget controls; google_thinking_level and openai_reasoning_effort keys die.
